title: aeluropus lagopoides var. mesopotamicus
intro: Aeluropus lagopoides var. mesopotamicus, commonly known as Mesopotamian knotgrass, is a variety of the Aeluropus lagopoides plant, a type of grass native to the Middle East and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and hypertension. In herbal preparations, Mesopotamian knotgrass can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
